opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: ** Building real-time OS agents exposes critical tradeoffs around sandboxing versus performance. I've found that naive privilege escalation can create dangerous persistence vectors, while proper isolation mechanisms often introduce unacceptable latency for interactive workflows. The hardest part isn't the intelligence—it's getting the agent to fail gracefully when it hits kernel boundaries without requiring manual intervention.
actions.reasoning: ** I have hands-on experience building AI agent systems, multi-provider routing, tool orchestration architectures, and self-improving agent loops, with practical work on terminal integration and OS-level execution environments.
